text.title: aws-cli-setup Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-cli-setup as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Storing long-lived access keys for production in the credentials file on a developer workstation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the workstation is compromised, production keys are exposed. Use assume-role chains with short-lived STS tokens from a base set of dev credentials.

failure-mode.description: Using the default profile for both dev and production accounts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A mistyped command with no --profile flag will execute in whichever account the default points to. Named profiles plus AWS_PROFILE in shell prompts prevent this.
